Sharpe Quadrant Study

Each scatter compares the Sharpe ratio across two time windows. Points above the diagonal improved over the longer horizon; points below were stronger recently. The SPY diamond defines the four quadrants — stocks in the top-right beat the benchmark on both periods.

Monte Carlo Simulation

Forward simulation of 1,000 portfolio paths over a 252-day horizon using three distributional assumptions. Block bootstrap preserves fat tails; Student-t adds heavier tails than the normal assumption.

Factor Exposure

Fama-French FF5 factor regression. Solid bars = significant (p<0.05). Error bars = ±1 SE. * p<0.1 ** p<0.05 *** p<0.01. Rolling betas (126-day window) show how factor exposures shift across regimes.
